net.EXO_29_36: Every day you are to prepare a bull for a purification offering for atonement. You are to purify the altar by making atonement for it, and you are to anoint it to set it apart as holy.
net.EXO_29_37: For seven days you are to make atonement for the altar and set it apart as holy. Then the altar will be most holy. Anything that touches the altar will be holy.
net.EXO_29_38: “Now this is what you are to prepare on the altar every day continually: two lambs a year old.
net.EXO_29_39: The first lamb you are to prepare in the morning, and the second lamb you are to prepare around sundown.
net.EXO_29_40: With the first lamb offer a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil from pressed olives, and a fourth of a hin of wine as a drink offering.
net.EXO_29_41: The second lamb you are to offer around sundown; you are to prepare for it the same meal offering as for the morning and the same drink offering, for a soothing aroma, an offering made by fire to the Lord.
net.EXO_29_42: “This will be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the Lord, where I will meet with you to speak to you there.
net.EXO_29_43: There I will meet with the Israelites, and it will be set apart as holy by my glory.
net.EXO_29_44: “So I will set apart as holy the tent of meeting and the altar, and I will set apart as holy Aaron and his sons that they may minister as priests to me.
net.EXO_29_45: I will reside among the Israelites, and I will be their God,
net.EXO_29_46: and they will know that I am the Lord their God, who brought them out from the land of Egypt, so that I may reside among them. I am the Lord their God.
net.EXO_30_1: “You are to make an altar for burning incense; you are to make it of acacia wood.
net.EXO_30_2: Its length is to be 18 inches and its width 18 inches; it will be square. Its height is to be 36 inches, with its horns of one piece with it.
net.EXO_30_3: You are to overlay it with pure gold—its top, its four walls, and its horns—and make a surrounding border of gold for it.
net.EXO_30_4: You are to make two gold rings for it under its border, on its two flanks; you are to make them on its two sides. The rings will be places for poles to carry it with.
net.EXO_30_5: You are to make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
net.EXO_30_6: “You are to put it in front of the curtain that is before the ark of the testimony (before the atonement lid that is over the testimony), where I will meet you.
net.EXO_30_7: Aaron is to burn sweet incense on it morning by morning; when he attends to the lamps he is to burn incense.
net.EXO_30_8: When Aaron sets up the lamps around sundown he is to burn incense on it; it is to be a regular incense offering before the Lord throughout your generations.
net.EXO_30_9: You must not offer strange incense on it, nor burnt offering, nor meal offering, and you must not pour out a drink offering on it.
net.EXO_30_10: Aaron is to make atonement on its horns once in the year with some of the blood of the sin offering for atonement; once in the year he is to make atonement on it throughout your generations. It is most holy to the Lord.” The Ransom Money
net.EXO_30_12: “When you take a census of the Israelites according to their number, then each man is to pay a ransom for his life to the Lord when you number them, so that there will be no plague among them when you number them.
net.EXO_30_13: Everyone who crosses over to those who are numbered is to pay this: a half shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary (a shekel weighs twenty gerahs). The half shekel is to be an offering to the Lord.
net.EXO_30_14: Everyone who crosses over to those numbered, from twenty years old and up, is to pay an offering to the Lord.
net.EXO_30_15: The rich are not to pay more and the poor are not to pay less than the half shekel when giving the offering of the Lord, to make atonement for your lives.
net.EXO_30_16: You are to receive the atonement money from the Israelites and give it for the service of the tent of meeting. It will be a memorial for the Israelites before the Lord, to make atonement for your lives.” The Bronze Laver
net.EXO_30_18: “You are also to make a large bronze basin with a bronze stand for washing. You are to put it between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it,
net.EXO_30_19: and Aaron and his sons must wash their hands and their feet from it.
net.EXO_30_20: When they enter the tent of meeting, they must wash with water so that they do not die. Also, when they approach the altar to minister by burning incense as an offering made by fire to the Lord,
net.EXO_30_21: they must wash their hands and their feet so that they do not die. And this will be a perpetual ordinance for them and for their descendants throughout their generations.” Oil and Incense
net.EXO_30_23: “Take choice spices: 12½ pounds of free-flowing myrrh, half that—about 6¼ pounds—of sweet-smelling cinnamon, 6¼ pounds of sweet-smelling cane,
net.EXO_30_24: and 12½ pounds of cassia, all weighed according to the sanctuary shekel, and four quarts of olive oil.
net.EXO_30_25: You are to make this into a sacred anointing oil, a perfumed compound, the work of a perfumer. It will be sacred anointing oil.
net.EXO_30_26: “With it you are to anoint the tent of meeting, the ark of the testimony,
net.EXO_30_27: the table and all its utensils, the lampstand and its utensils, the altar of incense,
net.EXO_30_28: the altar for the burnt offering and all its utensils, and the laver and its base.
net.EXO_30_29: So you are to sanctify them, and they will be most holy; anything that touches them will be holy.
net.EXO_30_30: “You are to anoint Aaron and his sons and sanctify them so that they may minister as my priests.
net.EXO_30_31: And you are to tell the Israelites: ‘This is to be my sacred anointing oil throughout your generations.
net.EXO_30_32: It must not be applied to people’s bodies, and you must not make any like it with the same recipe. It is holy, and it must be holy to you.
net.EXO_30_33: Whoever makes perfume like it and whoever puts any of it on someone not a priest will be cut off from his people.’”
net.EXO_30_34: The Lord said to Moses, “Take spices, gum resin, onycha, galbanum, and pure frankincense of equal amounts
net.EXO_30_35: and make it into an incense, a perfume, the work of a perfumer. It is to be finely ground, and pure and sacred.
net.EXO_30_36: You are to beat some of it very fine and put some of it before the ark of the testimony in the tent of meeting where I will meet with you; it is to be most holy to you.
net.EXO_30_37: And the incense that you are to make, you must not make for yourselves using the same recipe; it is to be most holy to you, belonging to the Lord.
net.EXO_30_38: Whoever makes anything like it, to use as perfume, will be cut off from his people.”
net.EXO_31_2: “See, I have chosen Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah,
net.EXO_31_3: and I have filled him with the Spirit of God in skill, in understanding, in knowledge, and in all kinds of craftsmanship,
net.EXO_31_4: to make artistic designs for work with gold, with silver, and with bronze,
net.EXO_31_5: and with cutting and setting stone, and with cutting wood, to work in all kinds of craftsmanship.
net.EXO_31_6: Moreover, I have also given him Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, and I have given ability to all the specially skilled, that they may make everything I have commanded you:
net.EXO_31_7: the tent of meeting, the ark of the testimony, the atonement lid that is on it, all the furnishings of the tent,
net.EXO_31_8: the table with its utensils, the pure lampstand with all its utensils, the altar of incense,
net.EXO_31_9: the altar for the burnt offering with all its utensils, the large basin with its base,
net.EXO_31_10: the woven garments, the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments for his sons, to minister as priests,
net.EXO_31_11: the anointing oil, and sweet incense for the Holy Place. They will make all these things just as I have commanded you.” Sabbath Observance
net.EXO_31_13: “Tell the Israelites, ‘Surely you must keep my Sabbaths, for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, that you may know that I am the Lord who sanctifies you.
net.EXO_31_14: So you must keep the Sabbath, for it is holy for you. Everyone who defiles it must surely be put to death; indeed, if anyone does any work on it, then that person will be cut off from among his people.
net.EXO_31_15: Six days work may be done, but on the seventh day is a Sabbath of complete rest, holy to the Lord; anyone who does work on the Sabbath day must surely be put to death.
net.EXO_31_16: The Israelites must keep the Sabbath by observing the Sabbath throughout their generations as a perpetual covenant.
net.EXO_31_17: It is a sign between me and the Israelites forever; for in six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth, and on the seventh day he rested and was refreshed.’”
net.EXO_31_18: He gave Moses two tablets of testimony when he had finished speaking with him on Mount Sinai, tablets of stone written by the finger of God.
net.EXO_32_1: When the people saw that Moses delayed in coming down from the mountain, they gathered around Aaron and said to him, “Get up, make us gods that will go before us. As for this fellow Moses, the man who brought us up from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him!”
net.EXO_32_2: So Aaron said to them, “Break off the gold earrings that are on the ears of your wives, your sons, and your daughters, and bring them to me.”
net.EXO_32_3: So all the people broke off the gold earrings that were on their ears and brought them to Aaron.
net.EXO_32_4: He accepted the gold from them, fashioned it with an engraving tool, and made a molten calf. Then they said, “These are your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of Egypt.”
net.EXO_32_5: When Aaron saw this, he built an altar before it, and Aaron made a proclamation and said, “Tomorrow will be a feast to the Lord.”
net.EXO_32_6: So they got up early on the next day and offered up burnt offerings and brought peace offerings, and the people sat down to eat and drink, and they rose up to play.
net.EXO_32_7: The Lord spoke to Moses, “Go quickly, descend, because your people, whom you brought up from the land of Egypt, have acted corruptly.
net.EXO_32_8: They have quickly turned aside from the way that I commanded them—they have made for themselves a molten calf and have bowed down to it and sacrificed to it and said, ‘These are your gods, O Israel, which brought you up from the land of Egypt.’”
net.EXO_32_9: Then the Lord said to Moses, “I have seen this people. Look what a stiff-necked people they are!
net.EXO_32_10: So now, leave me alone so that my anger can burn against them and I can destroy them, and I will make from you a great nation.”
net.EXO_32_11: But Moses sought the favor of the Lord his God and said, “O Lord, why does your anger burn against your people, whom you have brought out from the land of Egypt with great power and with a mighty hand?
net.EXO_32_12: Why should the Egyptians say, ‘For evil he led them out to kill them in the mountains and to destroy them from the face of the earth’? Turn from your burning anger, and relent of this evil against your people.
net.EXO_32_13: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel your servants, to whom you swore by yourself and told them, ‘I will multiply your descendants like the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have spoken about I will give to your descendants, and they will inherit it forever.’”
net.EXO_32_14: Then the Lord relented over the evil that he had said he would do to his people.
net.EXO_32_15: Moses turned and went down from the mountain with the two tablets of the testimony in his hands. The tablets were written on both sides—they were written on the front and on the back.
net.EXO_32_16: Now the tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, engraved on the tablets.
net.EXO_32_17: When Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said to Moses, “It is the sound of war in the camp!”
net.EXO_32_18: Moses said, “It is not the sound of those who shout for victory, nor is it the sound of those who cry because they are overcome, but the sound of singing I hear.”
net.EXO_32_19: When he approached the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, Moses became extremely angry. He threw the tablets from his hands and broke them to pieces at the bottom of the mountain.
net.EXO_32_20: He took the calf they had made and burned it in the fire, ground it to powder, poured it out on the water, and made the Israelites drink it.
net.EXO_32_21: Moses said to Aaron, “What did this people do to you, that you have brought on them so great a sin?”
net.EXO_32_22: Aaron said, “Do not let your anger burn hot, my lord; you know these people, that they tend to evil.
net.EXO_32_23: They said to me, ‘Make us gods that will go before us, for as for this fellow Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.’
net.EXO_32_24: So I said to them, ‘Whoever has gold, break it off.’ So they gave it to me, and I threw it into the fire, and this calf came out.”
net.EXO_32_25: Moses saw that the people were running wild, for Aaron had let them get completely out of control, causing derision from their enemies.
net.EXO_32_26: So Moses stood at the entrance of the camp and said, “Whoever is for the Lord, come to me.” All the Levites gathered around him,
net.EXO_32_27: and he said to them, “This is what the Lord, the God of Israel, has said ‘Each man fasten his sword on his side, and go back and forth from entrance to entrance throughout the camp, and each one kill his brother, his friend, and his neighbor.’”
net.EXO_32_28: The Levites did what Moses ordered, and that day about 3,000 men of the people died.
net.EXO_32_29: Moses said, “You have been consecrated today for the Lord, for each of you was against his son or against his brother, so he has given a blessing to you today.”
net.EXO_32_30: The next day Moses said to the people, “You have committed a very serious sin, but now I will go up to the Lord—perhaps I can make atonement on behalf of your sin.”
net.EXO_32_31: So Moses returned to the Lord and said, “Alas, this people has committed a very serious sin, and they have made for themselves gods of gold.
net.EXO_32_32: But now, if you will forgive their sin…, but if not, wipe me out from your book that you have written.”
net.EXO_32_33: The Lord said to Moses, “Whoever has sinned against me—that person I will wipe out of my book.
net.EXO_32_34: So now go, lead the people to the place I have spoken to you about. See, my angel will go before you. But on the day that I punish, I will indeed punish them for their sin.”
net.EXO_32_35: And the Lord sent a plague on the people because they had made the calf —the one Aaron made.
net.EXO_33_1: The Lord said to Moses, “Go up from here, you and the people whom you brought up out of the land of Egypt, to the land I promised on oath to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, ‘I will give it to your descendants.’
net.EXO_33_2: I will send an angel before you, and I will drive out the Canaanite, the Amorite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
net.EXO_33_3: Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey. But I will not go up among you, for you are a stiff-necked people, and I might destroy you on the way.”
net.EXO_33_4: When the people heard this troubling word they mourned; no one put on his ornaments.
net.EXO_33_5: For the Lord had said to Moses, “Tell the Israelites, ‘You are a stiff-necked people. If I went up among you for a moment, I might destroy you. Now take off your ornaments that I may know what I should do to you.’”
net.EXO_33_6: So the Israelites stripped off their ornaments by Mount Horeb. The Presence of the Lord
net.EXO_33_7: Moses took the tent and pitched it outside the camp, at a good distance from the camp, and he called it the tent of meeting. Anyone seeking the Lord would go out to the tent of meeting that was outside the camp.
net.EXO_33_8: And when Moses went out to the tent, all the people would get up and stand at the entrance to their tents and watch Moses until he entered the tent.
net.EXO_33_9: And whenever Moses entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the entrance of the tent, and the Lord would speak with Moses.
net.EXO_33_10: When all the people would see the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance of the tent, all the people, each one at the entrance of his own tent, would rise and worship.
net.EXO_33_11: The Lord would speak to Moses face to face, the way a person speaks to a friend. Then Moses would return to the camp, but his servant, Joshua son of Nun, a young man, did not leave the tent.
net.EXO_33_12: Moses said to the Lord, “See, you have been saying to me, ‘Bring this people up,’ but you have not let me know whom you will send with me. But you said, ‘I know you by name, and also you have found favor in my sight.’
net.EXO_33_13: Now if I have found favor in your sight, show me your way, that I may know you, that I may continue to find favor in your sight. And see that this nation is your people.”
net.EXO_33_14: And the Lord said, “My presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.”
net.EXO_33_15: And Moses said to him, “If your presence does not go with us, do not take us up from here.
net.EXO_33_16: For how will it be known then that I have found favor in your sight, I and your people? Is it not by your going with us, so that we will be distinguished, I and your people, from all the people who are on the face of the earth?”
net.EXO_33_17: The Lord said to Moses, “I will do this thing also that you have requested, for you have found favor in my sight, and I know you by name.”
net.EXO_33_18: And Moses said, “Show me your glory.”
net.EXO_33_19: And the Lord said, “I will make all my goodness pass before your face, and I will proclaim the Lord by name before you; I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious; I will show mercy to whom I will show mercy.”
net.EXO_33_20: But he added, “You cannot see my face, for no one can see me and live.”
net.EXO_33_21: The Lord said, “Here is a place by me; you will station yourself on a rock.
net.EXO_33_22: When my glory passes by, I will put you in a cleft in the rock and will cover you with my hand while I pass by.
net.EXO_33_23: Then I will take away my hand, and you will see my back, but my face must not be seen.”
net.EXO_34_1: The Lord said to Moses, “Cut out two tablets of stone like the first, and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets, which you smashed.
net.EXO_34_2: Be prepared in the morning, and go up in the morning to Mount Sinai, and station yourself for me there on the top of the mountain.
net.EXO_34_3: No one is to come up with you; do not let anyone be seen anywhere on the mountain; not even the flocks or the herds may graze in front of that mountain.”
net.EXO_34_4: So Moses cut out two tablets of stone like the first; early in the morning he went up to Mount Sinai, just as the Lord had commanded him, and he took in his hand the two tablets of stone.
net.EXO_34_5: The Lord descended in the cloud and stood with him there and proclaimed the Lord by name.
net.EXO_34_6: The Lord passed by before him and proclaimed: “The Lord, the Lord, the compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger, and abounding in loyal love and faithfulness,
net.EXO_34_7: keeping loyal love for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin. But he by no means leaves the guilty unpunished, responding to the transgression of fathers by dealing with children and children’s children, to the third and fourth generation.”
net.EXO_34_8: Moses quickly bowed to the ground and worshiped
net.EXO_34_9: and said, “If now I have found favor in your sight, O Lord, let my Lord go among us, for we are a stiff-necked people; pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for your inheritance.”
net.EXO_34_10: He said, “See, I am going to make a covenant before all your people. I will do wonders such as have not been done in all the earth, nor in any nation. All the people among whom you live will see the work of the Lord, for it is a fearful thing that I am doing with you.
net.EXO_34_11: “Obey what I am commanding you this day. I am going to drive out before you the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
net.EXO_34_12: Be careful not to make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land where you are going, lest it become a snare among you.
net.EXO_34_13: Rather you must destroy their altars, smash their images, and cut down their Asherah poles.
net.EXO_34_14: For you must not worship any other god, for the Lord, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.
net.EXO_34_15: Be careful not to make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, for when they prostitute themselves to their gods and sacrifice to their gods, and someone invites you, you will eat from his sacrifice;
net.EXO_34_16: and you then take his daughters for your sons, and when his daughters prostitute themselves to their gods, they will make your sons prostitute themselves to their gods as well.
net.EXO_34_17: You must not make yourselves molten gods.
net.EXO_34_18: “You must keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you must eat bread made without yeast, as I commanded you; do this at the appointed time of the month Abib, for in the month Abib you came out of Egypt.
net.EXO_34_19: “Every firstborn of the womb belongs to me, even every firstborn of your cattle that is a male, whether ox or sheep.
net.EXO_34_20: Now the firstling of a donkey you may redeem with a lamb, but if you do not redeem it, then break its neck. You must redeem all the firstborn of your sons. “No one will appear before me empty-handed.
net.EXO_34_21: “On six days you may labor, but on the seventh day you must rest; even at the time of plowing and of harvest you are to rest.
net.EXO_34_22: “You must observe the Feast of Weeks—the firstfruits of the harvest of wheat—and the Feast of Ingathering at the end of the year.
net.EXO_34_23: At three times in the year all your men must appear before the Sovereign Lord, the God of Israel.
net.EXO_34_24: For I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your borders; no one will covet your land when you go up to appear before the Lord your God three times in the year.
net.EXO_34_25: “You must not offer the blood of my sacrifice with yeast; the sacrifice from the Feast of Passover must not remain until the following morning.
net.EXO_34_26: “The first of the firstfruits of your soil you must bring to the house of the Lord your God. “You must not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
net.EXO_34_27: The Lord said to Moses, “Write down these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel.”
net.EXO_34_28: So he was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights; he did not eat bread, and he did not drink water. He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Commandments. The Radiant Face of Moses
net.EXO_34_29: Now when Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the testimony in his hand —when he came down from the mountain, Moses did not know that the skin of his face shone while he talked with him.
net.EXO_34_30: When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to approach him.
net.EXO_34_31: But Moses called to them, so Aaron and all the leaders of the community came back to him, and Moses spoke to them.
net.EXO_34_32: After this all the Israelites approached, and he commanded them all that the Lord had spoken to him on Mount Sinai.
net.EXO_34_33: When Moses finished speaking with them, he would put a veil on his face.
net.EXO_34_34: But when Moses went in before the Lord to speak with him, he would remove the veil until he came out. Then he would come out and tell the Israelites what he had been commanded.
net.EXO_34_35: When the Israelites would see the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses’ face shone, Moses would put the veil on his face again, until he went in to speak with the Lord.
net.EXO_35_1: Moses assembled the whole community of the Israelites and said to them, “These are the things that the Lord has commanded you to do.
net.EXO_35_2: In six days work may be done, but on the seventh day there must be a holy day for you, a Sabbath of complete rest to the Lord. Anyone who does work on it will be put to death.
net.EXO_35_3: You must not kindle a fire in any of your homes on the Sabbath day.” Willing Workers
net.EXO_35_4: Moses spoke to the whole community of the Israelites, “This is the word that the Lord has commanded:
net.EXO_35_5: ‘Take an offering for the Lord. Let everyone who has a willing heart bring an offering to the Lord: gold, silver, bronze;
net.EXO_35_6: blue, purple, and scarlet yarn; fine linen; goats’ hair;
net.EXO_35_7: ram skins dyed red; fine leather; acacia wood;
net.EXO_35_8: olive oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense;
net.EXO_35_9: onyx stones, and other gems for mounting on the ephod and the breastpiece.
net.EXO_35_10: Every skilled person among you is to come and make all that the Lord has commanded:
net.EXO_35_11: the tabernacle with its tent, its covering, its clasps, its frames, its crossbars, its posts, and its bases;
net.EXO_35_12: the ark, with its poles, the atonement lid, and the special curtain that conceals it;
net.EXO_35_13: the table with its poles and all its vessels, and the Bread of the Presence;
net.EXO_35_14: the lampstand for the light and its accessories, its lamps, and oil for the light;
net.EXO_35_15: and the altar of incense with its poles, the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense; the hanging for the door at the entrance of the tabernacle;
net.EXO_35_16: the altar for the burnt offering with its bronze grating that is on it, its poles, and all its utensils; the large basin and its pedestal;
net.EXO_35_17: the hangings of the courtyard, its posts and its bases, and the curtain for the gateway to the courtyard;
net.EXO_35_18: tent pegs for the tabernacle and tent pegs for the courtyard and their ropes;
net.EXO_35_19: the woven garments for serving in the Holy Place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments for his sons to minister as priests.’”
net.EXO_35_20: So the whole community of the Israelites went out from the presence of Moses.
net.EXO_35_21: Everyone whose heart stirred him to action and everyone whose spirit was willing came and brought the offering for the Lord for the work of the tent of meeting, for all its service, and for the holy garments.
net.EXO_35_22: They came, men and women alike, all who had willing hearts. They brought brooches, earrings, rings and ornaments, all kinds of gold jewelry, and everyone came who waved a wave offering of gold to the Lord.
net.EXO_35_23: Everyone who had blue, purple, or scarlet yarn, fine linen, goats’ hair, ram skins dyed red, or fine leather brought them.
net.EXO_35_24: Everyone making an offering of silver or bronze brought it as an offering to the Lord, and everyone who had acacia wood for any work of the service brought it.
net.EXO_35_25: Every woman who was skilled spun with her hands and brought what she had spun, blue, purple, or scarlet yarn, or fine linen,
net.EXO_35_26: and all the women whose heart stirred them to action and who were skilled spun goats’ hair.
net.EXO_35_27: The leaders brought onyx stones and other gems to be mounted for the ephod and the breastpiece,
net.EXO_35_28: and spices and olive oil for the light, for the anointing oil, and for the fragrant incense.
net.EXO_35_29: The Israelites brought a freewill offering to the Lord, every man and woman whose heart was willing to bring materials for all the work that the Lord through Moses had commanded them to do.
net.EXO_35_30: Moses said to the Israelites, “See, the Lord has chosen Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.
net.EXO_35_31: He has filled him with the Spirit of God—with skill, with understanding, with knowledge, and in all kinds of work—
net.EXO_35_32: to design artistic designs, to work in gold, in silver, and in bronze,
net.EXO_35_33: and in cutting stones for their setting, and in cutting wood, to do work in every artistic craft.
net.EXO_35_34: And he has put it in his heart to teach, he and Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan.
net.EXO_35_35: He has filled them with skill to do all kinds of work as craftsmen, as designers, as embroiderers in blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and in fine linen, and as weavers. They are craftsmen in all the work and artistic designers.
net.EXO_36_1: So Bezalel and Oholiab and every skilled person in whom the Lord has put skill and ability to know how to do all the work for the service of the sanctuary are to do the work according to all that the Lord has commanded.”
net.EXO_36_2: Moses summoned Bezalel and Oholiab and every skilled person in whom the Lord had put skill—everyone whose heart stirred him to volunteer to do the work.
net.EXO_36_3: They received from Moses all the offerings the Israelites had brought to do the work for the service of the sanctuary, and they still continued to bring him a freewill offering each morning.
net.EXO_36_4: So all the skilled people who were doing all the work on the sanctuary came from the work they were doing
net.EXO_36_5: and told Moses, “The people are bringing much more than is needed for the completion of the work which the Lord commanded us to do!”
net.EXO_36_6: Moses instructed them to take his message throughout the camp, saying, “Let no man or woman do anymore work for the offering for the sanctuary.” So the people were restrained from bringing any more.
net.EXO_36_7: Now the materials were more than enough for them to do all the work. The Building of the Tabernacle
net.EXO_36_8: All the skilled among those who were doing the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twisted linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarn; they were made with cherubim that were the work of an artistic designer.
net.EXO_36_9: The length of one curtain was 42 feet, and the width of one curtain was 6 feet—the same size for each of the curtains.
net.EXO_36_10: He joined five of the curtains to one another, and the other five curtains he joined to one another.
net.EXO_36_11: He made loops of blue material along the edge of the end curtain in the first set; he did the same along the edge of the end curtain in the second set.
net.EXO_36_12: He made fifty loops on the first curtain, and he made fifty loops on the end curtain that was in the second set, with the loops opposite one another.
net.EXO_36_13: He made fifty gold clasps and joined the curtains together to one another with the clasps, so that the tabernacle was a unit.
net.EXO_36_14: He made curtains of goats’ hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he made eleven curtains.
net.EXO_36_15: The length of one curtain was 45 feet, and the width of one curtain was 6 feet—one size for all eleven curtains.
net.EXO_36_16: He joined five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves.
net.EXO_36_17: He made fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in the first set and fifty loops along the edge of the curtain that joined the second set.
net.EXO_36_18: He made fifty bronze clasps to join the tent together so that it might be a unit.
net.EXO_36_19: He made a covering for the tent out of ram skins dyed red and over that a covering of fine leather.
net.EXO_36_20: He made the frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood as uprights.
net.EXO_36_21: The length of each frame was 15 feet, the width of each frame was 2¼ feet,
net.EXO_36_22: with two projections per frame parallel one to another. He made all the frames of the tabernacle in this way.
net.EXO_36_23: So he made frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side.
net.EXO_36_24: He made forty silver bases under the twenty frames—two bases under the first frame for its two projections, and likewise two bases under the next frame for its two projections,
net.EXO_36_25: and for the second side of the tabernacle, the north side, he made twenty frames
net.EXO_36_26: and their forty silver bases, two bases under the first frame and two bases under the next frame.
net.EXO_36_27: And for the back of the tabernacle on the west he made six frames.
net.EXO_36_28: He made two frames for the corners of the tabernacle on the back.
net.EXO_36_29: At the two corners they were doubled at the lower end and finished together at the top in one ring. So he did for both.
net.EXO_36_30: So there were eight frames and their silver bases, sixteen bases, two bases under each frame.
net.EXO_36_31: He made bars of acacia wood, five for the frames on one side of the tabernacle
net.EXO_36_32: and five bars for the frames on the second side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the frames of the tabernacle for the back side on the west.
net.EXO_36_33: He made the middle bar to reach from end to end in the center of the frames.
net.EXO_36_34: He overlaid the frames with gold and made their rings of gold to provide places for the bars, and he overlaid the bars with gold.
net.EXO_36_35: He made the special curtain of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine twisted linen; he made it with cherubim, the work of an artistic designer.
net.EXO_36_36: He made for it four posts of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold, with gold hooks, and he cast for them four silver bases.
net.EXO_36_37: He made a hanging for the entrance of the tent of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine twisted linen, the work of an embroiderer,
net.EXO_36_38: and its five posts and their hooks. He overlaid their tops and their bands with gold, but their five bases were bronze.
net.EXO_37_1: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood; its length was 45 inches, its width 27 inches, and its height 27 inches.
net.EXO_37_2: He overlaid it with pure gold, inside and out, and he made a surrounding border of gold for it.
net.EXO_37_3: He cast four gold rings for it that he put on its four feet, with two rings on one side and two rings on the other side.
net.EXO_37_4: He made poles of acacia wood, overlaid them with gold,
net.EXO_37_5: and put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark in order to carry the ark.
net.EXO_37_6: He made an atonement lid of pure gold; its length was 45 inches, and its width was 27 inches.
net.EXO_37_7: He made two cherubim of gold; he made them of hammered metal on the two ends of the atonement lid,
net.EXO_37_8: one cherub on one end and one cherub on the other end. He made the cherubim from the atonement lid on its two ends.
net.EXO_37_9: The cherubim were spreading their wings upward, overshadowing the atonement lid with their wings. The cherubim faced each other, looking toward the atonement lid. The Making of the Table
net.EXO_37_10: Bezalel made the table of acacia wood; its length was 36 inches, its width 18 inches, and its height 27 inches.
net.EXO_37_11: He overlaid it with pure gold, and he made a surrounding border of gold for it.
net.EXO_37_12: He made a surrounding frame for it about three inches wide, and he made a surrounding border of gold for its frame.
net.EXO_37_13: He cast four gold rings for it and attached the rings at the four corners where its four legs were.
net.EXO_37_14: The rings were close to the frame to provide places for the poles to carry the table.
net.EXO_37_15: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold, to carry the table.
net.EXO_37_16: He made the vessels which were on the table out of pure gold, its plates, its ladles, its pitchers, and its bowls, to be used in pouring out offerings. The Making of the Lampstand
net.EXO_37_17: Bezalel made the lampstand of pure gold. He made the lampstand of hammered metal; its base and its shaft, its cups, its buds, and its blossoms were from the same piece.
net.EXO_37_18: Six branches were extending from its sides, three branches of the lampstand from one side of it, and three branches of the lampstand from the other side of it.
net.EXO_37_19: Three cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms were on the first branch, and three cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms were on the next branch, and the same for the six branches that were extending from the lampstand.
net.EXO_37_20: On the lampstand there were four cups shaped like almond flowers with buds and blossoms,
net.EXO_37_21: with a bud under the first two branches from it, and a bud under the next two branches from it, and a bud under the third two branches from it; according to the six branches that extended from it.
net.EXO_37_22: Their buds and their branches were of one piece; all of it was one hammered piece of pure gold.
net.EXO_37_23: He made its seven lamps, its trimmers, and its trays of pure gold.
net.EXO_37_24: He made the lampstand and all its accessories with seventy-five pounds of pure gold. The Making of the Altar of Incense
net.EXO_37_25: Bezalel made the incense altar of acacia wood. Its length was 18 inches and its width 18 inches—a square—and its height was 36 inches. Its horns were of one piece with it.
net.EXO_37_26: He overlaid it with pure gold—its top, its four walls, and its horns—and he made a surrounding border of gold for it.
net.EXO_37_27: He also made two gold rings for it under its border, on its two sides, on opposite sides, as places for poles to carry it with.
net.EXO_37_28: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
net.EXO_37_29: He made the sacred anointing oil and the pure fragrant incense, the work of a perfumer.
net.EXO_38_1: Bezalel made the altar for the burnt offering of acacia wood 7½ feet long and 7½ feet wide—it was square—and its height was 4½ feet.
net.EXO_38_2: He made its horns on its four corners; its horns were part of it, and he overlaid it with bronze.
net.EXO_38_3: He made all the utensils of the altar—the pots, the shovels, the tossing bowls, the meat hooks, and the fire pans—he made all its utensils of bronze.
net.EXO_38_4: He made a grating for the altar, a network of bronze under its ledge, halfway up from the bottom.
net.EXO_38_5: He cast four rings for the four corners of the bronze grating, to provide places for the poles.
net.EXO_38_6: He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
net.EXO_38_7: He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar, with which to carry it. He made the altar hollow, out of boards.
net.EXO_38_8: He made the large basin of bronze and its pedestal of bronze from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance of the tent of meeting. The Construction of the Courtyard
net.EXO_38_9: Bezalel made the courtyard. For the south side the hangings of the courtyard were of fine twisted linen, 150 feet long,
net.EXO_38_10: with their twenty posts and their twenty bronze bases, with the hooks of the posts and their bands of silver.
net.EXO_38_11: For the north side the hangings were 150 feet, with their twenty posts and their twenty bronze bases, with the hooks of the posts and their bands of silver.
net.EXO_38_12: For the west side there were hangings 75 feet long, with their ten posts and their ten bases, with the hooks of the posts and their bands of silver.
net.EXO_38_13: For the east side, toward the sunrise, it was 75 feet wide,
net.EXO_38_14: with hangings on one side of the gate that were 22½ feet long, with their three posts and their three bases,
net.EXO_38_15: and for the second side of the gate of the courtyard, just like the other, the hangings were 22½ feet long, with their three posts and their three bases.
net.EXO_38_16: All the hangings around the courtyard were of fine twisted linen.
net.EXO_38_17: The bases for the posts were bronze. The hooks of the posts and their bands were silver, their tops were overlaid with silver, and all the posts of the courtyard had silver bands.
net.EXO_38_18: The curtain for the gate of the courtyard was of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine twisted linen, the work of an embroiderer. It was 30 feet long and, like the hangings in the courtyard, it was 7½ feet high,
net.EXO_38_19: with four posts and their four bronze bases. Their hooks and their bands were silver, and their tops were overlaid with silver.
net.EXO_38_20: All the tent pegs of the tabernacle and of the courtyard all around were bronze. The Materials of the Construction
net.EXO_38_21: This is the inventory of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the testimony, which was counted by the order of Moses, being the work of the Levites under the direction of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.
net.EXO_38_22: Now Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made everything that the Lord had commanded Moses;
net.EXO_38_23: and with him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an artisan, a designer, and an embroiderer in blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine linen.
net.EXO_38_24: All the gold that was used for the work, in all the work of the sanctuary (namely, the gold of the wave offering) was 29 talents and 730 shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.
net.EXO_38_25: The silver of those who were numbered of the community was 100 talents and 1,775 shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel,
net.EXO_38_26: one beka per person, that is, a half shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel, for everyone who crossed over to those numbered, from twenty years old or older, 603,550 in all.
net.EXO_38_27: The 100 talents of silver were used for casting the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the special curtain—100 bases for 100 talents, one talent per base.
net.EXO_38_28: From the remaining 1,775 shekels he made hooks for the posts, overlaid their tops, and made bands for them.
net.EXO_38_29: The bronze of the wave offering was seventy talents and 2,400 shekels.
net.EXO_38_30: With it he made the bases for the door of the tent of meeting, the bronze altar, the bronze grating for it, and all the utensils of the altar,
net.EXO_38_31: the bases for the courtyard all around, the bases for the gate of the courtyard, all the tent pegs of the tabernacle, and all the tent pegs of the courtyard all around.
net.EXO_39_1: From the blue, purple, and scarlet yarn they made woven garments for serving in the sanctuary; they made holy garments that were for Aaron, just as the Lord had commanded Moses. The Ephod
net.EXO_39_2: He made the ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet yarn, and fine twisted linen.
net.EXO_39_3: They hammered the gold into thin sheets and cut it into narrow strips to weave them into the blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and into the fine linen, the work of an artistic designer.
net.EXO_39_4: They made shoulder pieces for it, attached to two of its corners, so it could be joined together.
net.EXO_39_5: The artistically woven waistband of the ephod that was on it was like it, of one piece with it, of gold, blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and fine twisted linen, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
net.EXO_39_6: They set the onyx stones in gold filigree settings, engraved as with the engravings of a seal with the names of the sons of Israel.
net.EXO_39_7: He put them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as stones of memorial for the Israelites, just as the Lord had commanded Moses. The Breastpiece of Decision
net.EXO_39_8: He made the breastpiece, the work of an artistic designer, in the same fashion as the ephod, of gold, blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, and fine twisted linen.
net.EXO_39_9: It was square—they made the breastpiece doubled, nine inches long and nine inches wide when doubled.
net.EXO_39_10: They set on it four rows of stones: a row with a ruby, a topaz, and a beryl—the first row;
net.EXO_39_11: and the second row, a turquoise, a sapphire, and an emerald;
net.EXO_39_12: and the third row, a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
net.EXO_39_13: and the fourth row, a chrysolite, an onyx, and a jasper. They were enclosed in gold filigree settings.
net.EXO_39_14: The stones were for the names of the sons of Israel, twelve, corresponding to the number of their names. Each name corresponding to one of the twelve tribes was like the engravings of a seal.
net.EXO_39_15: They made for the breastpiece braided chains like cords of pure gold,
net.EXO_39_16: and they made two gold filigree settings and two gold rings, and they attached the two rings to the upper two ends of the breastpiece.
net.EXO_39_17: They attached the two gold chains to the two rings at the ends of the breastpiece;
net.EXO_39_18: the other two ends of the two chains they attached to the two settings, and they attached them to the shoulder pieces of the ephod at the front of it.
net.EXO_39_19: They made two rings of gold and put them on the other two ends of the breastpiece on its edge, which is on the inner side of the ephod.
net.EXO_39_20: They made two more gold rings and attached them to the bottom of the two shoulder pieces on the front of the ephod, close to the juncture above the waistband of the ephod.
net.EXO_39_21: They tied the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod by blue cord, so that it was above the waistband of the ephod, so that the breastpiece would not be loose from the ephod, just as the Lord had commanded Moses. The Other Garments
net.EXO_39_22: He made the robe of the ephod completely blue, the work of a weaver.
net.EXO_39_23: There was an opening in the center of the robe, like the opening of a collar, with an edge all around the opening so that it could not be torn.
net.EXO_39_24: They made pomegranates of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn and twisted linen around the hem of the robe.
net.EXO_39_25: They made bells of pure gold and attached the bells between the pomegranates around the hem of the robe between the pomegranates.
net.EXO_39_26: There was a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, all around the hem of the robe, to be used in ministering, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
net.EXO_39_27: They made tunics of fine linen—the work of a weaver, for Aaron and for his sons—
net.EXO_39_28: and the turban of fine linen, the headbands of fine linen, and the undergarments of fine twisted linen.
net.EXO_39_29: The sash was of fine twisted linen and blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, the work of an embroiderer, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
net.EXO_39_30: They made a plate, the holy diadem, of pure gold and wrote on it an inscription, as on the engravings of a seal, “Holiness to the Lord.”
net.EXO_39_31: They attached to it a blue cord to attach it to the turban above, just as the Lord had commanded Moses. Moses Inspects the Tabernacle
net.EXO_39_32: So all the work of the tabernacle, the tent of meeting, was completed, and the Israelites did according to all that the Lord had commanded Moses—they did it exactly so.
net.EXO_39_33: They brought the tabernacle to Moses, the tent and all its furnishings, clasps, frames, bars, posts, and bases;
net.EXO_39_34: and the coverings of ram skins dyed red, the covering of fine leather, and the protecting curtain;
net.EXO_39_35: the ark of the testimony and its poles, and the atonement lid;
net.EXO_39_36: the table, all its utensils, and the Bread of the Presence;
net.EXO_39_37: the pure lampstand, its lamps, with the lamps set in order, and all its accessories, and oil for the light;
net.EXO_39_38: and the gold altar, and the anointing oil, and the fragrant incense; and the curtain for the entrance to the tent;
net.EXO_39_39: the bronze altar and its bronze grating, its poles, and all its utensils; the large basin with its pedestal;
net.EXO_39_40: the hangings of the courtyard, its posts and its bases, and the curtain for the gateway of the courtyard, its ropes and its tent pegs, and all the furnishings for the service of the tabernacle, for the tent of meeting;
net.EXO_39_41: the woven garments for serving in the sanctuary, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments for his sons to minister as priests.
net.EXO_39_42: The Israelites did all the work according to all that the Lord had commanded Moses.
net.EXO_39_43: Moses inspected all the work, and they had done it just as the Lord had commanded—they had done it exactly—and Moses blessed them.
net.EXO_40_1: Then the Lord spoke to Moses:
net.EXO_40_2: “On the first day of the first month you are to set up the tabernacle, the tent of meeting.
net.EXO_40_3: You are to place the ark of the testimony in it and shield the ark with the special curtain.
net.EXO_40_4: You are to bring in the table and set out the things that belong on it; then you are to bring in the lampstand and set up its lamps.
net.EXO_40_5: You are to put the gold altar for incense in front of the ark of the testimony and put the curtain at the entrance to the tabernacle.
net.EXO_40_6: You are to put the altar for the burnt offering in front of the entrance to the tabernacle, the tent of meeting.
net.EXO_40_7: You are to put the large basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it.
net.EXO_40_8: You are to set up the courtyard around it and put the curtain at the gate of the courtyard.
net.EXO_40_9: And take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it, and sanctify it and all its furnishings, and it will be holy.
net.EXO_40_10: Then you are to anoint the altar for the burnt offering with all its utensils; you are to sanctify the altar, and it will be the most holy altar.
net.EXO_40_11: You must also anoint the large basin and its pedestal, and you are to sanctify it.
net.EXO_40_12: “You are to bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
net.EXO_40_13: Then you are to clothe Aaron with the holy garments and anoint him and sanctify him so that he may minister as my priest.
net.EXO_40_14: You are to bring his sons and clothe them with tunics
net.EXO_40_15: and anoint them just as you anointed their father, so that they may minister as my priests; their anointing will make them a priesthood that will continue throughout their generations.”
net.EXO_40_16: This is what Moses did, according to all the Lord had commanded him—so he did.
net.EXO_40_17: So the tabernacle was set up on the first day of the first month, in the second year.
net.EXO_40_18: When Moses set up the tabernacle and put its bases in place, he set up its frames, attached its bars, and set up its posts.
net.EXO_40_19: Then he spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent over it, as the Lord had commanded him.
net.EXO_40_20: He took the testimony and put it in the ark, attached the poles to the ark, and then put the atonement lid on the ark.
net.EXO_40_21: And he brought the ark into the tabernacle, hung the protecting curtain, and shielded the ark of the testimony from view, just as the Lord had commanded him.
net.EXO_40_22: And Moses put the table in the tent of meeting, on the north side of the tabernacle, outside the curtain.
net.EXO_40_23: And he set the bread in order on it before the Lord, just as the Lord had commanded him.
net.EXO_40_24: And he put the lampstand in the tent of meeting opposite the table, on the south side of the tabernacle.
net.EXO_40_25: Then he set up the lamps before the Lord, just as the Lord had commanded him.
net.EXO_40_26: And he put the gold altar in the tent of meeting in front of the curtain,
net.EXO_40_27: and he burned fragrant incense on it, just as the Lord had commanded him.
net.EXO_40_28: Then Moses put the curtain at the entrance to the tabernacle.
net.EXO_40_29: He also put the altar for the burnt offering by the entrance to the tabernacle, the tent of meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the meal offering, just as the Lord had commanded him.
net.EXO_40_30: Then he put the large basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
net.EXO_40_31: Moses and Aaron and his sons would wash their hands and their feet from it.
net.EXO_40_32: Whenever they entered the tent of meeting, and whenever they approached the altar, they would wash, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
net.EXO_40_33: And he set up the courtyard around the tabernacle and the altar, and put the curtain at the gate of the courtyard. So Moses finished the work.
net.EXO_40_34: Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
net.EXO_40_35: Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud settled on it and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
net.EXO_40_36: But when the cloud was lifted up from the tabernacle, the Israelites would set out on all their journeys;
net.EXO_40_37: but if the cloud was not lifted up, then they would not journey farther until the day it was lifted up.
net.EXO_40_38: For the cloud of the Lord was on the tabernacle by day, but fire would be on it at night, in plain view of all the house of Israel throughout all their journeys.
